Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Cody Bollinger And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanction In The Sum Of $2,021.05.; Memorandum Of Points And Authoritie
Off Calendar Oct-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. A previous stipulation is on file dated 10/19/23. The Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Cody Bollinger And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanction In The Sum Of $2,021.05.

The motion is granted, including the request for sanctions in the amount of $2,000 payable no later than November 15, 2023. Ms. Kirkgaard, to prepare the proposed order approved as to form directly to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com with a copy to Discovery302@sftc.org.

Judge Pro Tem: William C. Lynn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Cody Bollinger And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanction In The Sum Of $2,021.05.; Memorandum Of Points And Authoritie
Off Calendar Oct-19-2023 Continued To Oct-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Susan E. Kirkgaard, Esg. of Law Offices of John A. Biard, tel. 916.638.6610, Email: skirkgaa@travlers.com for Plaintiff Travelers Casualty Company of America;
David Barch, Esq. of Armijo Morovati & Shields, Tel. 415.273.6503, Email: david.barch@marmijolaw.com, for Defendant Patterson Pump Company;
Taha Majeed, Esq. of London Fischer, Tel. 949.252.0550, for Defendant Patterson Pump Company.
Hearing on: PLAINTIFF TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Cody Bollinger And Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanction In The Sum Of $2,021.05.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Argument presented by counsel for both sides.

At the conclusion of argument, the matter is continued to October 20, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. on the discovery calendar. The parties are to meet and confer on dates for the deposition and costs incurred by the moving party. If the parties reach an agreement they may send a proposed order with their agreed upon language to Judge Pro Tem Lynn for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Not Present. Matter not reported. (JPT/302)
